name: Jay Mohr
blurb: The Jersey impressionist's impressionist — Walken, Pacino, Tracy Morgan, Norm — wrapped around two-and-a-half years of SNL trauma turned into the best memoir an SNL cast member has ever written.
bio: Jay Mohr was the kid from Verona, New Jersey who hit the MTV Half Hour Comedy Hour at 20 and was a featured player on Saturday Night Live by 23 — a two-year run he chronicled later in Gasping for Airtime, the most candid book any cast member has written about the Lorne Michaels machine. On screen he is Bob Sugar in Jerry Maguire and Peter Dragon in Action; on stage he is a pure impressionist's comic whose Christopher Walken, Al Pacino, Tracy Morgan, and Norm Macdonald are the load-bearing pillars of any set. Five stand-up specials between Funny For a Girl (2012) and Altamont (2021), with Happy. And A Lot. picking up a Grammy nomination for Best Comedy Album, plus a long second act as Fox Sports Radio's midday host and a fixture on The Jim Rome Show.

timeline.ev: Born August 23 in Verona, New Jersey
MTV's Half Hour Comedy Hour debut at 20
Joins Saturday Night Live as a featured player (seasons 19–20)
Plays Bob Sugar in Jerry Maguire — breakout film role
Stars as Peter Dragon in the cult Fox sitcom Action
Creates, hosts, and executive-produces NBC's Last Comic Standing (Primetime Emmy nomination)
Publishes Gasping for Airtime: Two Years in the Trenches of Saturday Night Live
Showtime special Funny For a Girl — his first hour in seven years
Happy. And A Lot. nominated for the Grammy for Best Comedy Album
Comedy Dynamics releases Jay Mohr: American Treasure
Jay Mohr: Altamont special — material written after a stint in rehab for Adderall addiction

accolades: Grammy nomination — Best Comedy Album (Happy. And A Lot., 58th Annual Grammy Awards, 2015)
Primetime Emmy nomination — Outstanding Reality Program (Last Comic Standing, as creator/host/EP)
CableACE Award for hosting MTV's Lip Service
Author of Gasping for Airtime (Hyperion, 2004) and No Wonder My Parents Drank (Simon & Schuster, 2010)

specials.t: Jay Mohr: Happy. And A Lot.
Jay Mohr: Funny For a Girl

ranking_notes: The Tracy Morgan impression is the canonical Mohr bit candidate — it is the impression every other impressionist concedes to him. The full Altamont half-hour is the strongest argument for putting him above the impression-act-only stereotype.
